refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability that meets or exceeds the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on achieving a specified availability target. It is solution-neutral and clearly states what the system must do without implying how. Minor refinement was done to clarify the time frame and maintain neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability that meets or exceeds the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design system components with redundancy to ensure continuous operation during failures
Implement real-time monitoring and diagnostics to detect and address faults promptly
Establish maintenance and repair procedures to minimize downtime
Incorporate failover mechanisms to switch to backup systems automatically
Define operational policies and safety plans that support availability targets
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time monitoring and diagnostics to detect and address faults promptly

potential_sources: Dependency between monitoring and repair procedures if not well modularized
Tight coupling between failover mechanisms and component design
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on detection and addressing faults, which can be modularized independently from redundancy and maintenance procedures, supporting decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Availability target is achievable given system complexity and environment
Operational policies are effectively enforced and followed
Monitoring systems themselves are reliable and do not introduce new failure modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating DPs related to redundancy and failover mechanisms to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for monitoring system health, diagnostics, and fault management to ensure operational safety.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from various system components for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts and notifications when faults or safety issues are detected.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules and external systems for data exchange and coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of monitoring logic.
Contains physical components and some monitoring, but monitoring and diagnostics are not its primary focus, causing moderate diffusion.
Focused on remote control and tasking; monitoring and diagnostics are peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Specialized in safety-related monitoring and diagnostics; high cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing real-time monitoring and diagnostics because it specializes in safety and fault management, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from other modules.
Risk of overloading Safety Services with non-safety-related monitoring tasks if scope is not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment issues arise, consider refining the DP to focus specifically on safety-critical monitoring and diagnostics rather than all real-time monitoring, to better align with Safety Services scope.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time monitoring and diagnostics directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability targets by enabling prompt fault detection and resolution, which supports maintaining or exceeding the availability threshold.

diffusion_risks: Monitoring and diagnostics logic is diffused across multiple modules such as NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation, which have moderate to high diffusion scores.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating it is the most appropriate IM boundary for implementing the DP with minimal diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring Safety Services outputs such as SafetyAlert notifications and telemetry data to confirm fault detection and response effectiveness, which supports measuring operational availability against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services module is clear and aligns well with the module's scope of monitoring system health and fault management, ensuring ownership and accountability.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP scope to focus specifically on safety-critical monitoring and diagnostics to better align with the Safety Services module and avoid overloading it with non-safety-related monitoring tasks.
Establish clear interface contracts and data dependencies with other modules (e.g., Telemetry input) to mitigate risks related to timely and accurate data delivery.
Consider modularizing or consolidating monitoring functions currently spread across other modules to reduce diffusion and improve cohesion within Safety Services.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the recommended revisions highlight important refinements needed to better align the DP and IM with the safety-critical nature of the system and to reduce diffusion risks. These refinements include focusing the DP scope on safety-critical monitoring, establishing clear interface contracts, and consolidating monitoring functions to improve cohesion. Addressing these revisions before proceeding ensures a more robust and maintainable design that meets high safety criticality requirements.

revise.why: To refine the DP scope specifically for safety-critical monitoring and diagnostics and to consolidate and modularize monitoring functions within the Safety Services module, reducing diffusion and improving cohesion.

assumptions: The Safety Services module is the appropriate IM boundary for implementing the DP with minimal diffusion risk.
Verification of operational availability through Safety Services outputs is feasible and effective.
open_risks: Diffusion of monitoring and diagnostics logic across multiple modules may impact maintainability and safety.
Timely and accurate data delivery from other modules (e.g., Telemetry) is critical and requires clear interface contracts.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming that refined DP and IM implementations meet operational availability targets.
Interface contract definitions and data dependency mappings between Safety Services and other modules.
